title: Prove AUT(A) is countable (finite-support automorphisms only)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Sub-lemma (G2).** Prove that the constructed $A$ has *countable* automorphism group, as required for the framing (Theorems 2.2 and 2.3 both assume $\AUT(A)$ countable).

Key danger: with infinitely many independent binary choices one naively gets $\AUT(A)\cong(\Z/2)^{\N}$, which is **uncountable**. The construction must instead force every automorphism to act nontrivially on only *finitely many* gadgets, so that
$$\AUT(A)\cong\bigoplus_n \AUT(G_n)\quad(\text{direct sum, countable}),$$
rather than the direct product. Typically this needs a 'linking/coordination' relation that forces finite support — but the lemma must verify this linking does **not** (a) create new unexpected automorphisms, nor (b) rigidify $A$ so much that the diagonal clause (G4) becomes impossible.

Deliverable: a complete characterization of $\AUT(A)$ and a proof it is countable.
